action.hypothesis: Round 23 showed that increasing `AUXK_ALPHA` above the `topk/signum/K128/EF12/LR=1.6e-3` reference did not improve the anchor recipe, which suggests the mainline is likely saturated on auxiliary-loss magnitude itself. This round changes only `DEAD_FEATURE_THRESHOLD`, lowering it from the effectively inactive `10000000` to `100000`, to test whether earlier dead-feature rescue improves late-stage reconstruction or curve shape without changing the core topk recipe.
action.summary: Run a single loss-schedule test on the exact topk mainline reference recipe: keep `ARCHITECTURE=topk`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=1.6e-3`, `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125` fixed, and reduce `DEAD_FEATURE_THRESHOLD` to `100000` to see whether activating auxiliary rescue earlier helps the K128 baseline.
action.self_review: This stays on the active `topk` family, changes only one non-core variable on the exact reference backbone, respects the fixed `EF=12` rule, and follows directly from the evidence that both weaker and stronger `AUXK_ALPHA` failed to beat the baseline. The main risk is that the threshold change may have little effect over this training horizon, but it is the cleanest remaining single-variable way to probe whether the auxiliary mechanism is mis-timed rather than mis-scaled.
notes_to_memory: Round 14 (`AUXK_ALPHA=0.015625`) and Round 23 (`AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625`) both failed to improve the K128 anchor, so the mainline loss axis now looks saturated on magnitude.
The next clean question is whether the auxiliary dead-feature mechanism is mistimed rather than misweighted; the current threshold `10000000` is effectively inactive over this run length.

Judge this run by full curve shape as well as final FVU: if earlier rescue smooths late descent or lowers FVU, the issue was activation timing; if not, the current topk/signum/K128 recipe is likely saturated on the aux-loss path.
next_hypotheses: If lowering `DEAD_FEATURE_THRESHOLD` improves the K128 reference, continue refining that schedule on the same backbone before moving to other recipe axes.
If earlier dead-feature rescue does not help, treat the current loss path as saturated and move to a different single recipe variable.
If lowering the threshold hurts stability or reconstruction, keep the effectively inactive dead-feature schedule as the better mainline choice.

result.description: Run a single loss-schedule test on the exact topk mainline reference recipe: keep `ARCHITECTURE=topk`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=1.6e-3`, `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125` fixed, and reduce `DEAD_FEATURE_THRESHOLD` to `100000` to see whether activating auxiliary rescue earlier helps the K128 baseline.
result.self_review: This stays on the active `topk` family, changes only one non-core variable on the exact reference backbone, respects the fixed `EF=12` rule, and follows directly from the evidence that both weaker and stronger `AUXK_ALPHA` failed to beat the baseline. The main risk is that the threshold change may have little effect over this training horizon, but it is the cleanest remaining single-variable way to probe whether the auxiliary mechanism is mis-timed rather than mis-scaled.
